scope: Pass B full-surface expansion. CoinMarketCap organizes its documented Pro API surface into Cryptocurrency (19 endpoints), Exchange (7), Global Metrics (6), Content (4), Community (2), CMC Index (4), Tools (4), Derivatives (3), Crypto Others (1), DEX Data (16+ across Token/Platform/Holder/OHLCV/Others), and a Deprecated bucket (21). Every list endpoint whose real wire pagination is CoinMarketCap's documented 1-based `start`/`limit` convention (start advances by page size each page: 1, 1+pageSize, 2*pageSize+1, ...) is excluded as an ENGINE_GAP -- neither `page_number` (advances the page value by +1, never by page size) nor `offset_limit` (Start() is hardcoded to offset 0 with no start-offset override field) can express this shape; unchanged finding from the original quarantine, re-verified against every S3/S4 dialect addition (fan_out, keyed_object, extra_params, start_page:0, typed computed_fields) -- see docs.md Known limits. Every non-paginated id/slug/symbol-keyed detail lookup and every parameterless/optionally-filtered single-object endpoint that IS dialect-expressible is implemented as a stream (9 streams total, up from 1). Sibling id-keyed detail endpoints in the same families as the newly covered streams (ohlcv, quotes/historical, price-performance-stats, exchange info/quotes, category/airdrop singular lookups) are marked out_of_scope with the specific reason they were not implemented THIS pass rather than covered -- narrowing scope honestly instead of silently omitting them from the surface manifest. DEX Data, Derivatives, CMC Index, Content/Community, and the Deprecated bucket are out of scope for this pass as distinct, separately-scoped API surfaces (on-chain data, index/derivatives products, retired partnerships) unrelated to this connector's core fiat-market-data streams.
